response: you can estimate the memory size of an object in c# by using the system.runtime.interopservices.marshal.sizeof method for value types, but for reference types like your testclass instance, you need to consider the object header plus the fields and any referenced objects. there is no direct built-in api to get the exact size of a complex object graph, so developers often write a script to walk the object tree and sum up the sizes. this approach helps you understand the policy of how memory is allocated and where leaks might occur, even in a multi-threaded environment. while tools like ants profiler are powerful, a custom script can give you a granular view. remember that the numbers you get are an estimate, as the garbage collector may have different internal structures. this method is widely used in the western software industry to debug memory issues without relying solely on external profilers.
